posts.facebook:
Most founders I talk to are stuck between two bad options: a slow agency burning R400k+ or a junior dev who delivers something that breaks in production.

There is a third option. Senior engineering, project-based, no equity split.

I work with 2-3 founders at a time. From idea to live product, without the overhead.

qwabi.co.za

instagram.caption: Senior engineering for founders who can't afford to get it wrong. qwabi.co.za #TaaS #StartupSA #SoftwareEngineering #FounderLife

posts.facebook:
My first step on every project is not code. It's a PRD.

Writing forces the clarity that conversation never does. Before I touch a database schema or a UI, we have a document that answers: What are we building? Who for? What is the smallest version that proves the idea?

Most junior devs skip this. That's why you end up 3 months in with something that solves the wrong problem.

instagram.caption: Documentation first. Code second. Always. #ProductEngineering #MVP #TechFounder #BuildInPublic

posts.facebook:
What I actually do as a Technical Co-founder as a Service:

- Own the full architecture decision
- Write and review production code
- Integrate payments, auth, notifications
- Set up cloud infra and CI/CD
- Ship something testable in weeks, not months

If you are pre-seed or bootstrapped with a real product to build, message me.

qwabi.co.za

instagram.caption: From idea to live product. That's the job. qwabi.co.za #SeniorDev #StartupLife #AfricanTech #CodeAfrica

posts.facebook:
The Junior Dev Lottery is real.

You hire cheap, you get: no tests, no documentation, broken payment flows on launch day, and a codebase no one else can maintain.

The hidden cost is not the rate. It is the rebuild 6 months later when the product fails under real users.

Senior engineering is not a luxury. It is insurance.

instagram.caption: Cheap code is the most expensive mistake a founder makes. #JuniorDevLottery #StartupMistakes #TechFounder

posts.facebook:
I built a multi-app ride-hailing ecosystem (Rider, Driver, Admin, Media platform) in a single week.

Not a prototype. A working, testable product with auth, real-time location, and payment integration.

This is what AI force multiplication looks like in practice. One senior engineer directing parallel agents. Zero meeting overhead. Full stack output.

trip.qwabi.co.za

instagram.caption: Rider app. Driver app. Admin. Media platform. One week. This is what AI force multiplication looks like. #BuildInPublic #AfricanTech #AI

posts.facebook:
My stack for most South African products:

Frontend: React Native (Expo) for mobile, Next.js or Vite/React for web
Backend: Node.js, Supabase, or Clojure for complex domains
Payments: Paystack (SA-native, not Stripe)
Infra: Vercel, Railway, or GCP depending on the scale
AI: OpenAI or local models where data privacy matters

Every choice is Business-Right, not just technically interesting.

instagram.caption: Stack choices that work in the SA context. Paystack, Supabase, Expo. Built for Africa. #AfricanTech #TechStack #ReactNative

posts.facebook:
A founder came to me with a clinic management system that an agency built for R280k.

It could not generate a patient report without crashing.

We rebuilt the core in 3 weeks, added the features they actually needed, and launched. The business has been running it in production for 8 months.

That is what Business-Right engineering looks like.

instagram.caption: R280k agency build. Crashed on patient reports. Rebuilt in 3 weeks. Running in production 8 months later. #ProductEngineering #AfricanTech

posts.facebook:
What founders actually need from a technical partner:

1. Someone who understands the business goal, not just the feature request
2. Visible progress early and often, not a big reveal
3. A product they can sell, not a liability they have to fix
4. Direct communication, no account managers

That is the pitch. If it fits your situation, qwabi.co.za

instagram.caption: You need a builder who understands the business, not just the tech. qwabi.co.za #TaaS #FounderLife #Startup

posts.facebook:
Most MVPs fail before they meet a real user.

Not because of bad design. Because of bad assumptions baked into the data model on week one.

If your schema is wrong, your product is wrong. Everything built on top of it is borrowed time.

This is why the PRD and data model come before any UI work in my process.

instagram.caption: Wrong data model = wrong product. The foundation matters more than the design. #MVP #DataModel #Engineering

posts.facebook:
I built a full laundry marketplace: Customer app, Driver app, Partner dashboard for laundromats, Admin dashboard.

React Native. Supabase. Paystack. Real-time order tracking. Scheduled pickups.

One person. One stack. Live in South Africa.

laundry.qwabi.co.za

instagram.caption: Customer app. Driver app. Laundromat dashboard. Admin panel. One stack, live in SA. laundry.qwabi.co.za #BuildInPublic #ReactNative #AfricanStartup

posts.facebook:
The question I ask every founder on our first call:

"What happens to your business if this product does not exist in 12 months?"

If the answer is nothing, we are building a nice-to-have. If the answer is real pain, we are building something worth investing in.

Clarity on this question changes everything about how we scope, prioritize, and build.

instagram.caption: The one question that clarifies every product decision. What happens if this doesn't get built? #ProductThinking #Startup #FounderMindset

posts.facebook:
Hiring a CTO vs working with me:

CTO: 12+ months to hire, equity, R1M+ comp, may not fit early-stage culture
Me: Direct start, project-based, senior architecture and code, no equity

I am not a replacement for a CTO long-term. But for the first 12-18 months while you are validating product-market fit, the math is straightforward.

instagram.caption: TaaS vs hiring a CTO. The math is different at the early stage. #TechFounder #StartupAdvice #CTO #AfricanStartup

posts.facebook:
I work in Clojure for backend systems that need to last.

Not a popular choice. But functional programming, immutable data, and a REPL-driven workflow produces code that is genuinely easier to maintain 3 years later.

Most client projects use Node.js or Python for speed. Clojure is what I reach for when correctness and longevity matter more than hiring a big team.

instagram.caption: Functional programming isn't trendy. It's just more correct. #Clojure #FunctionalProgramming #BackendDev #Engineering

posts.facebook:
Three signals that a founder is ready to work with me:

1. They can explain the business problem clearly, not just the features
2. They understand that quality takes some real investment
3. They want progress they can see, not a big reveal after months

If that is you, the next step is a scoping call. qwabi.co.za

instagram.caption: Ready to build? Three things tell me we're a good fit. qwabi.co.za #TaaS #Founder #TechPartner

posts.facebook:
AI is not replacing senior engineers. It is replacing the time senior engineers spend on scaffolding.

I use Cursor, custom agents, and prompt chains to handle boilerplate, file generation, and routine code. That frees me to focus on architecture, business logic, and quality.

The output: a small team's velocity with one engineer's communication overhead.

instagram.caption: AI removes the scaffolding. Senior engineers handle the architecture. That's the combination. #AIEngineering #CursorAI #BuildFaster

posts.facebook:
The most underrated thing about working with a senior engineer:

They push back.

A junior will build exactly what you asked for. A senior will tell you when what you asked for is the wrong thing to build.

That conversation, before the code is written, is worth more than any feature.

instagram.caption: The best engineers don't just build what you ask for. They tell you when you're asking for the wrong thing. #SeniorDev #ProductThinking

posts.facebook:
Agencies are not bad. They are optimized for different problems.

An agency makes sense when: you have a large team, a long-term retainer, and a complex marketing operation.

I make sense when: you need a production-ready product built fast, with someone who owns the architecture and can make technical decisions without a committee.

Know which problem you actually have.

instagram.caption: Agency vs TaaS. Different tools for different problems. Know which one you need. #Freelance #Agency #StartupAdvice

posts.facebook:
10 years of building in South Africa has taught me:

- Mobile-first is not optional. SA users live on phones.
- Paystack beats Stripe for local payment success rates.
- Load shedding means offline-capable features are a real product requirement.
- Data costs matter. Your app cannot assume fast, cheap bandwidth.

Building for Africa means building for African realities.

instagram.caption: Building for Africa means building for African realities. Mobile-first. Paystack. Load-shedding resilient. #AfricanTech #BuildForAfrica #TechSA

posts.facebook:
What a 30% deposit actually signals:

Not just payment. It is a mutual commitment that we are both serious.

Clients who pay a deposit show up to calls. They provide feedback. They make decisions. They treat the project like the business investment it is.

Clients who want to start without one almost always want to stop without warning too.

instagram.caption: Commitment signals matter before the first line of code. #FreelanceLife #ClientManagement #Contracts #StartupAdvice

posts.facebook:
I am currently open for one new project this quarter.

Good fits: non-technical founders who have validated a real problem, agencies needing a reliable white-label technical partner, SMEs ready to move off manual workflows.

Not a fit: clients who want hourly billing, spec changes after sign-off, or to start without a deposit.

qwabi.co.za to start the conversation.

instagram.caption: One slot open this quarter. Is your project a good fit? qwabi.co.za #TaaS #HireaDev #AfricanStartup #SoftwareEngineering

posts.facebook:
Shipping is not the end of the product. It is the beginning of the real work.

Post-launch: monitoring, user feedback loops, performance optimization, feature iterations based on real data.

Most junior devs disappear after launch. Most agencies start the next project.

I offer iteration retainers precisely because a product is never done.

instagram.caption: Launch is not the finish line. It's where the real product work begins. #ProductEngineering #PostLaunch #Iteration

posts.facebook:
I use TypeScript for everything now. Not optional.

Strict types eliminate an entire category of runtime bugs before they reach production. For a small team or a solo engineer, type safety is not pedantry. It is your test suite when you do not have five QA engineers.

If your current codebase is untyped JS, that is a liability you are carrying every day.

instagram.caption: TypeScript is not optional. Strict types are your first line of defense in a small team. #TypeScript #WebDev #EngineeringBestPractices

posts.facebook:
What I look for in a project before I take it:

- A real business problem, not a feature list
- A founder who can make decisions quickly
- A budget that signals they are serious
- A timeline that is ambitious but not delusional

If your project fits, I will give it everything. If it does not, I will tell you clearly and point you somewhere better.

instagram.caption: I only take projects I can win. Here's what I look for before I say yes. #Consulting #TaaS #ClientSelection

posts.facebook:
The real cost of staying on WhatsApp:

- Orders lost because someone did not see a message
- No transaction history, no analytics, no audit trail
- Customer expectations you cannot meet at scale
- Zero credibility with investors or enterprise clients

Your business is bigger than an Instagram link. Time to build the platform.

instagram.caption: Your business is bigger than your Instagram link. Time to build the platform. #Digitize #SmallBusiness #DigitalTransformation #AfricanBusiness

posts.facebook:
I worked remotely from Queenstown for a Cape Town company for over a year.

What I learned: over-communication is not optional. You have to write things down. You have to take notes that save the next person 2 hours. You have to show progress before anyone asks.

These habits make me a better builder for remote-first founders everywhere.

instagram.caption: Remote work taught me that written communication is a technical skill. Notes save lives. #RemoteWork #Engineering #Productivity
instagram.query: remote work home office laptop coffee

posts.facebook:
My pricing is not hourly.

Hourly billing creates the wrong incentives: slow = more money. That is backwards.

I scope projects at a fixed Phase 1 price based on scope, complexity, and timeline. You know what you are getting and what it costs before we start.

For ongoing work, there are monthly retainers. No surprises.

qwabi.co.za

instagram.caption: Fixed price. Clear scope. No hourly billing surprises. That's how I work. qwabi.co.za #Pricing #Freelance #TaaS
